records.ety: [L. provisio: cf. F. provision. See Provide.]

defs: The act of providing, or making previous preparation.
That which is provided or prepared; that which is brought together or arranged in advance; measures taken beforehand; preparation.
quotes: Making provision for the relief of strangers.

defs: Especially, a stock of food; any kind of eatables collected or stored; -- often in the plural.
quotes: And of provisions laid in large, For man and beast.

defs: That which is stipulated in advance; a condition; a previous agreement; a proviso; as, the provisions of a contract; the statute has many provisions.
A canonical term for regular induction into a benefice, comprehending nomination, collation, and installation.
A nomination by the pope to a benefice before it became vacant, depriving the patron of his right of presentation.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Provisioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Provisioning.]

defs: To supply with food; to victual; as, to provision a garrison.
quotes: They were provisioned for a journey.
